name: Funk Filter
explanation: An envelope-follower auto-wah: a resonant state-variable filter whose cutoff is swept by the input's own dynamics. Play louder and the peak swoops further; a peak-detecting envelope follower with snappy attack and a musical release tail drives the sweep. Direction flips it (up: louder opens the filter; down: louder closes it), Mode picks a low-pass or a vocal band-pass voice, Sensitivity sets how hard the envelope pushes, Resonance sets the Q/peak, and Range sets the span of the sweep. The classic funky, vocal 'wow' that tracks your playing. Controls: Sensitivity, Resonance, Range, Direction, Mode and dry/wet Mix.
